content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals from ICM-based autonomous agents can be repurposed to rank uncertainty for memory sample selection in deep active agents, creating a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent's innate desire to explore unknowns prioritizes storing high-impact memory samples that further boost active learning performance. This hybrid system replaces static uncertainty scoring with a dynamic, exploration-aligned ranking that adapts to the agent's current knowledge gap.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
